name: Project.Feature.Packaging.BsToolBundling_Feature
observations: BsTool.exe bundled as binary resource in PyInstaller spec file for automatic inclusion in packaged executable.
Path detection uses hybrid approach: sys._MEIPASS (temp extraction) → sys.executable directory (dist folder) → project root (development).
Eliminates need for separate BsTool.exe distribution or manual path configuration.

name: Project.Method.Packaging.HybridResourcePathDetection_Method
observations: Implemented in BsToolCommandService._get_bstool_path() with three-tier fallback strategy.
Tier 1: Check sys._MEIPASS for PyInstaller onefile mode (temp extraction directory).
Tier 2: Use sys.executable directory for onedir mode (distribution folder).
Tier 3: Fallback to project root for development environment.

name: Project.Configuration.Packaging.WindowsServer2012Manifest_Configuration
observations: Custom Windows manifest XML in LOGReporter.spec with explicit supportedOS declarations.
Includes Windows Server 2012 ({4a2f28e3-53b9-4441-ba9c-d69d4a4a6e38}) and Server 2012 R2 ({d78f2640-1f3f-11e3-8fae-00144feabdc0}).
Covers Vista/2008 through Windows 11 and all Server versions for maximum compatibility.
Applied via manifest parameter in PyInstaller EXE class.
